div.head: 112. Editorial Note
div.p: On May 7, representatives of the Governments of France and the United States signed an agreement at Washington entitled “French-United States Cooperation in the Uses of Atomic Energy.” It provided for the sale to the Government of France of a specified quantity of enriched uranium for use in the development and operation of a land-based prototype submarine nuclear propulsion plant during a 10-year period. For text of this agreement, see 10 UST 1279. Documentation on the agreement is in Department of State, Central Files, 751.5621 /4–259.
